title: Quadratic collision / broad-phase blow-up
symptoms: collision.broadphase cost grows faster than the object count
broadphase or narrow-phase pair checks explode
n^2 pair testing when many objects cluster together
hitch scales with the square of active colliders
mechanism: Collision detection degenerates toward O(n^2) pair tests, often when many colliders overlap a region or no spatial partition is used, so broad-phase cost spikes.
diagnosis_hints: Check whether the collider count grew modestly but collision cost grew much more; look for clustering of objects in one area.
typical_fixes: use a spatial partition / grid / BVH for the broad phase
set collision layers and masks to cut irrelevant pairs
reduce overlapping colliders or simplify shapes
